forms.name: Referral for examination by psychiatrist
forms.purpose: Use when a person needs referral for examination by a psychiatrist after assessment.
forms.maker: Medical practitioner or authorised mental health practitioner where the Act permits.
forms.involved: Receiving service, transport team if separately authorised, personal support contacts where required.
forms.threshold: Reasonable suspicion that involuntary treatment order criteria may be met, with illness features, risk, capacity and least restrictive reasoning documented.
forms.clock: Usually 72 hours from referral. The referral is made within the required assessment window.
forms.destination: Examination place must be clear and align with any detention or transport authority.
forms.authorises: Referral for examination by a psychiatrist.
forms.doesNotAuthorise: Treatment, detention, transport, restraint, seclusion or force by itself.
forms.copies: Use approved form pathway; include confidential attachment only where required.
forms.documentationStem: Assessed at [time/place]. Reasonably suspect need for involuntary treatment order because [illness features], [risk], [capacity issue] and [least restrictive reasoning]. Examination destination: [place].
traps: Treating Form 1A as detention, treatment, transport or force authority.
Destination mismatch between referral and transport order.
Capacity reasoning replaced by “poor insight” or “refused treatment”.
forms.safetyPearl: Separate referral authority from any detention, transport or treatment authority.
forms.sourceNote: Source-derived summary from supplied Form 1A guide and WA MHA forms workflow notes.
forms.legalNote: Confirm the referral assessment, destination/place and expiry before any downstream detention, transport or treatment step; referral is not itself treatment, detention, transport or force authority. Use only the current approved form or PSOLIS pathway where available; only appropriately authorised people should complete MHA 2014 forms. Authority is limited to the Act sections, signature block, time limits and wording on the approved form.
practicePearls: Separate referral authority from any detention, transport or treatment authority.
Keep referral, detention, transport and treatment authorities separate in the record.
Clock cue: within 48 hours, 72 hours. Record the start time, expiry or review point, and next owner.
Source cue: sections 26, 31, 36, 37, 41, 42.
preUseChecks: Before use: confirm the assessment basis, examination place, referral expiry and any linked detention or transport form.
Confirm the patient/person identifiers, maker, date, time, place and signature fields are complete.
Confirm copies, notices, support-person communication and filing/PSOLIS requirements before handover.

forms.involved: Receiving hospital, transport lead, referring team.
forms.threshold: Existing referral remains live and variation or extension criteria are met.
forms.clock: Extension must be actioned before referral expiry where applicable.
forms.destination: Updated destination must be visible to the receiving team.
forms.authorises: Variation or extension of the referral pathway as specified.
forms.doesNotAuthorise: A new detention or transport power by itself.

forms.documentationStem: Existing referral reviewed at [time]. Variation/extension required because [reason]. Updated destination/expiry: [details].

forms.name: Detain voluntary inpatient for assessment
forms.purpose: Use for a voluntary inpatient requesting discharge where urgent assessment is required.
forms.maker: Authorised clinician specified by the Act pathway.
forms.involved: Ward medical team, nursing coordinator, psychiatrist or registrar.
forms.threshold: Assessment is required and no lawful next step has yet been completed.
forms.clock: Maximum 6 hours. Treat as a hard stop.
forms.destination: Adult inpatient ward or relevant hospital setting.
forms.authorises: Short assessment hold for the voluntary inpatient pathway.
forms.doesNotAuthorise: Ongoing detention beyond the maximum period or treatment without separate authority.
forms.copies: Hand over expiry and next owner clearly.
forms.documentationStem: Voluntary inpatient requested discharge at [time]. Form 2 commenced because [assessment reason]. Expiry and next lawful step documented.
traps: Missing the 6-hour hard stop.
Static detention without active assessment.
Not documenting release/off-ramp.
forms.safetyPearl: The expiry is the clinical priority. Decide and document the next lawful step early.
forms.sourceNote: Representative summary from supplied forms guide.
forms.legalNote: Detention authority must match the live referral or examination pathway, place, duration and expiry; do not use detention wording as treatment or transport authority. Use only the current approved form or PSOLIS pathway where available; only appropriately authorised people should complete MHA 2014 forms. Authority is limited to the Act sections, signature block, time limits and wording on the approved form.
practicePearls: The expiry is the clinical priority. Decide and document the next lawful step early.
The lawful clock and destination are the safety-critical parts of detention forms.
Clock cue: 6 hours, up to 6 hours. Record the start time, expiry or review point, and next owner.
Source cue: sections 34, 35.
preUseChecks: Before use: confirm live referral/order, detention place, expiry, receiving service and release/off-ramp trigger.
Confirm the patient/person identifiers, maker, date, time, place and signature fields are complete.
Confirm copies, notices, support-person communication and filing/PSOLIS requirements before handover.

forms.authorises: Detention for the specified pre-examination pathway.
forms.doesNotAuthorise: Transport by itself unless transport authority also exists.

forms.documentationStem: Live referral confirmed. Detention required because [reason]. Destination, expiry and responsible team documented.

forms.name: Transport order
forms.purpose: Use where a person must be conveyed and no other safe means is reasonably available.
forms.maker: Authorised maker specified by the transport pathway.
forms.involved: Transport provider, receiving service, referrer.
forms.threshold: Transport is necessary and no less restrictive safe means is reasonably available.
forms.clock: Must remain aligned with live referral/detention pathway.
forms.destination: Exact destination is essential and must match the referral pathway.
forms.authorises: Transport/conveyance according to the order.
forms.doesNotAuthorise: Psychiatric treatment or detention beyond the linked authority.
forms.copies: Notify transport and receiving service of destination and authority limits.
forms.documentationStem: Transport required because [reason] and no other safe means is reasonably available. Destination: [place]. Linked authority: [form].
traps: Destination mismatch.
Assuming 1A alone permits transport.
Not checking less restrictive travel option.
forms.safetyPearl: Transport is a separate legal step, not an automatic consequence of referral.
forms.sourceNote: Representative summary from supplied workflow guide.
forms.legalNote: Transport or transfer authority should be specific to the journey, destination and linked live authority, with no safer less restrictive option available. Use only the current approved form or PSOLIS pathway where available; only appropriately authorised people should complete MHA 2014 forms. Authority is limited to the Act sections, signature block, time limits and wording on the approved form.
practicePearls: Transport is a separate legal step, not an automatic consequence of referral.
A transport form should make the route, destination and linked authority easy to audit.
Clock cue: 72 hours. Record the start time, expiry or review point, and next owner.
preUseChecks: Before use: confirm origin, destination, transport provider, linked form, expiry and less restrictive travel options.
Confirm the patient/person identifiers, maker, date, time, place and signature fields are complete.

forms.name: Community treatment order
forms.purpose: Use where compulsory treatment can be safely managed in the community.
forms.maker: Psychiatrist or authorised pathway decision-maker.
forms.involved: Community mental health team, treating psychiatrist, support persons where required.
forms.threshold: Treatment order criteria met and community treatment is the least restrictive suitable pathway.
forms.clock: Review and continuation dates must be diarised.
forms.destination: Community treatment setting and responsible service must be clear.
forms.authorises: Compulsory treatment in the community according to CTO conditions.
forms.doesNotAuthorise: Inpatient detention unless a separate pathway applies.
forms.copies: Provide order details and conditions to relevant parties according to local process.
forms.documentationStem: CTO considered least restrictive because [community supports/risks]. Conditions and responsible service documented.
traps: Using inpatient logic without community feasibility analysis.
Conditions not communicated.
forms.safetyPearl: Least restrictive reasoning should be visible, not implied.
forms.sourceNote: Representative summary from supplied forms master guide.
forms.indexedClock: Up to 3 months, within 72 hours, not more than 3 months, within 14 days, 7 days, within 7 days
forms.legalNote: Treatment order forms should show criteria, least restrictive reasoning, authorised setting, review timing and required notices. Use only the current approved form or PSOLIS pathway where available; only appropriately authorised people should complete MHA 2014 forms. Authority is limited to the Act sections, signature block, time limits and wording on the approved form.
practicePearls: Least restrictive reasoning should be visible, not implied.

pathways.title: Referral for psychiatrist examination
pathways.scenario: Need psychiatrist exam after assessment in community, ED or ward.
pathways.risk: Do not treat Form 1A as detention, transport or force authority.

pathways.title: Voluntary inpatient wants to leave
pathways.scenario: Voluntary inpatient requests discharge and urgent assessment is required.
pathways.risk: Form 2 maximum 6 hours. Decide and document the next lawful step early.

pathways.title: General hospital pathway
pathways.scenario: Inpatient treatment in a general hospital due physical-health transfer risk.
pathways.risk: Chief Psychiatrist consent and physical-health transfer risk are the hinge points.

pathways.title: Restrictive practice event
pathways.scenario: Bodily restraint or seclusion episode requiring order, observation and follow-up.
pathways.risk: Keep restraint and seclusion clocks separate.

pathways.title: Communication restriction
pathways.scenario: Need to restrict a communication right and review before the next 24-hour period.
pathways.risk: A restriction should not drift past the review clock.

checklistGroups.title: Final form safety checklist
items: Current legal status is named.
Maker authority matches the selected form.
Threshold is explicit and fact-linked.
Clock or expiry is visible.
Destination and receiving team are clear.
Least restrictive option considered.

checklistGroups.title: Documentation checklist
items: Purpose and form pathway stated.
Capacity reasoning is decision-specific.
Risk is linked to mental illness features.
What the form authorises is separated from what it does not authorise.
Next owner and review time documented.

checklistGroups.title: Copy / notification checklist
items: Patient informed where required.
Support person or nominated person steps considered.
Receiving service has form and destination details.
Confidential attachment used only where appropriate.
PSOLIS or approved local record updated.

checklistGroups.title: Common traps
items: Form expired before action completed.
Referral confused with detention or transport authority.
Destination mismatch across linked forms.
Review clock not handed over.
Family awareness assumed but notification step missed.

Introduction to the Mental Health Act 2014 (MHA14) 
 
The MHA14 received Royal Assent on 3 November 2014 and will commence on 30 November 2015. It 
will replace the Mental Health Act 1996 which will remain in force until 29 November 2015. 
 
All clinicians must make every effort to comply with the Charter of Mental Health Care Principles when 
providing treatment, care and support to people experiencing mental illness. The Charter can be found in

sourceDocuments.title: MHA Charter Principles
sourceDocuments.excerpt:
We must protect and uphold the fundamental human rights of people experiencing mental 
illness and act in accordance with the national and international standards that apply to mental 
health services.
We must be easily accessible and safe and provide people experiencing mental illness with timely 
treatment, care and support of high quality based on contemporary best practice to promote 
recovery in the least restrictive manner that is consistent with their needs.
We must recognise, and be sensitive and responsive to, diverse individual circumstances, 
including those relating to gender, sexuality, age, family, disability, lifestyle choices

Information Sharing
The Mental Health Act 2014 (the Act) commenced on 
30 November 2015. The Act enables clinicians to share 
certain information about a patient without the need for 
consent and without breaching confidentiality laws or 
professional ethics or standards.

sourceDocuments.excerpt:
1 
 
MENTAL HEALTH ACT 2014 
Further Opinions 
 
What is a further opinion? 
If you have been admitted to hospital as an involuntary patient , or placed on a community 
treatment order, your psychiatrist will have developed and started a treatment plan that he 
or she t hinks will help you to recover from mental illness. If you do not agree with the 
treatment for your mental illness, you are entitled to request a further opinion. 
Also, if you are on a community treatment order and your supervising psychia trist has just 
continued the order, you can request a further opinion as to whether or not the order should 
have been continued.

Restrictive Practices Factsheet Restrictive practices in this context refers to the use of restraint on, and/or detention of, a patient. Key Principles Personal liberty is the most fundamental human right. Interference with a ty is only permitted where it is justified, authorised or excused by law.
